free_text: Fusion and Nonfusion Processes in Reactions Induced
By 10-100 MeV Li-6 Ions with Fe Targets.
The Extracted Beam Energy was Known Within +-0.3 MeV.
The Thicknesses of All Targets and Catchers were
Determined by Measuring the Energy Degradiation of
Alpha Particles from an Am-241 Source.
From the Measurements the Contributions of Various
Reaction Mechanisms Could be Identified and Also be
Determined the Extent to Which Various Mechanisms are
Involved in the Production of Different Final Nuclei.
Nekw
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
Reaction Lines are Corrected. Converted to
Lower Case.
Last checking has been done.
. In-Beam Gammas, Listed in Ind.Univ.Cycl.Rept.78-01
Fe-56, Isotopically Enriched Self-Supporting Targets,
Thickness About 3 Mg/Cm**2. Contributions of Other
Fe Isotopes were Less Than 0.08%.
The Detector was Placed About 3 Cm from the Target
And at 90 Degrees with Respect to the Beam. To Look
For Angular Distribution Effects Additional
Measurements were Made at 125 Degrees For Two
Bombarding Energies and Found to be Insignificant.
Spectra were Collected During 1-2 Hours.
Cross Sections For the Production of Individual Nuclei
Were Obtained by Summing Observed Cross Sections For
Transitions Leading to the Ground State, or in Some
Cases to Low Lying Excited States. Corrections For
Sidefeeding to the Ground-State were not Attempted.
Systematic Errors are Estimated to be About
15%. Detailed Error Analysis is Missing.
For Dead Time Losses were Included. Corrections For
Radioactivity Contribution were Found to be Negligible.

A Contribution of the Overlapping 2+ to 3+ Transition
Of Co-56 to the 810.6 KeV Peak was Estimated from the
Observed Intensities of Other Transitions of Co-56.

The Observed 2+ to 0+ Transition Overlaps with the
13/2 - To 11/2 - Transition in Co-57. The Production
Cross Section For Cr-54 is Assumed to be 1.7 Times
The Cross Section For Its 4+ to 2+ Transition. The
Factor 1.7 is Based Upon the Corresponding Systematics
Of Other Even-Even Nuclei.
The Contribution of the Ni-58 4+ to 2+ Transition to
The 1005.1 KeV Peak was Estimated Using the Observed
Intensity of Its 2+ to 0+ Transition.
. In-Beam Gammas, Listed in Ind.Univ.Cycl.Rept.78-01
The 11/2- To 7/2- Transition (E=1609.3KEV) In V-51
Was Used.

Half-Lives, Energies, and Branching Ratios were Taken
From W.W.Bowman and K.W.Mac Murdo, at.Data.Nucl. Data
Fe-56, Isotopically Enriched Self-Supporting Targets,
Thickness of Fe Foils About 4 Mg/Cm**2. Contributions
Of Other Fe Isotopes were Less Than 0.08%.
The Target Assemblies Consisted of Stacks of Fe Foils
And Al or Kapton Catchers, Interspersed with Al Beam
Energy Degraders. For Each Bombardment Up to Twelve
New Target-Catcher Combinations were Used.
Gamma-Spectra were Recorded Beginning About 10
Minutes  After the End of Bombardment and were Followed
During 3 Months.
The Total Production Cross Section of a Given Reaction
Product was Obtained by Summing the Cross Sections
Determined Separately from Target and Catcher
Activities. For All But the Shortest Lived Products
At Least Four Independent Activity Measurements were
Made.
The Quoted Errors were Estimated from the Spread in
The Values of the Cross Section Obtained Through
Different Measurements For All Relevant Transitions.
Detailed Informations About the Error Analysis are
Missing (COMMENT BY THE COMPILER OF KACHAPAG).
Half-Lives, Energies, and Branching Ratios were Taken
From W.W.Bowman and K.W.Mac Murdo, at.Data.Nucl. Data

That the Cross Section is Independent.
